headline: Head football coach, Dunbar High School
Retired USPS (32-year career)
Retired school administrator
Owner, Main Street Kitchen (Silver Spring)
First grader, Rosemont Elementary
Toddler, Little Explorers Daycare
Labor & delivery nurse, Riverside Memorial
Supervisor, Baltimore City DSS
Therapist, LCSW (private practice)
Orthopedist, GBMC
Primary care physician, Bon Secours
Pediatrician, Pediatric Associates of Baltimore
Pediatric hematologist, City Medical Center
Primary care physician, GBMC
Dentist, Downtown Family Dental
Pastor, Grace Community Church
Assistant football coach & PE teacher, Dunbar HS
Primary care physician, Bon Secours
CPA, Whitfield Tax & Accounting
Family (Dakar)
Rosemont Elementary School
Little Explorers Daycare
CPS home visit ran into the evening; safety assessment done.
PRIDE foster cohort starts Oct 6 — adapting the curriculum with my case studies.
Court testimony at Juvenile Court this morning, then back to the caseload.
Sunday pot roast from Dad's recipe; mafe next weekend from Uncle Oscar's playbook.
Choir rehearsal Thursday — alto, half therapy and half worship.
Nightly journal, one Moleskine a year, above the desk I rarely reread.
Wednesday dinner with Mom, six to eight-thirty, standing.
Therapy with Dr. Grant — court and removals are the triggers.
Raised beds out back, okra because Dad planted it, Fiona narrating the herbs.
Book club Saturday — I'm the one who finished the book again.
Marcus's Dunbar team looked sharp; David running the soccer carpool.
Andrew's teacher flagged attention concerns; monitoring, no eval yet.
Fiona's thyroid panel repeat on the 22nd, watching it.
Denise cooked tonight and ran the kitchen, spoiling the grandkids.
Caseload at 20 families; recommended max is 15, the constant overload.
Reset playlist for the hard nights — gospel and two Tom Petty songs.
Foster cohort roster in Sheets; 14 graduated last cohort.
Back PT twice a week; delaying the microdiscectomy for the caseload.
Uncle Oscar's Main Street Kitchen for the monthly family dinner.
Buspirone steady, Calm app three or four nights a week.
Secondary-trauma debrief with Brenda; the bureaucracy constrains her.
Senegal folder in email — almost-planning Dakar with the kids for ten years.
Lexington Market crab cakes, El Salvadoreno pupusas in Fells Point.
Front-stoop coffee chair in warm weather, the morning quiet.
